common_assumptions: File state is unchanged between consecutive tool calls within a session
A single-directory or single-file search is sufficient to establish absence of a pattern
Error messages reflect root cause without deeper tracing through callsites
Prior session context is fully recoverable from WAL or checkpoint files without independent verification
strength_patterns: Iterative verification loops: large chains consistently run type-checkers or diagnostic scripts (tsc --noEmit, statusline-diag.py) after each edit batch before committing, catching regressions in-flight
Context-first orientation: chains that handle complex tasks reliably open by reading WAL, runtime-notes, or checkpoint files before any side-effecting operation
Targeted multi-file fan-out: when diagnosing failures, chains spread across config files, logs, and settings rather than fixating on a single suspected source
weakness_patterns: Fixation without re-framing: the longest chains repeat the same script or file 20-30+ times without stepping back to question the diagnosis (f3ddfd2f: 30+ statusline.sh invocations across a 110-step chain)

Shallow verdict chains: juror and summarization tasks resolve in 0-1 steps with no evidence of weighing alternatives or grounding claims in file:line citations, producing structurally underspecified outputs

Linear single-hypothesis execution: nearly all chains pursue one hypothesis to completion before reconsidering, generating high assumption density on the critical path with no explicit branching on uncertainty
